root_text.sn56.55:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, puriso mahāpathaviyā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā upanikkhipeyya.
root_text.sn56.55:1.3: katamaṁ nu kho bahutaraṁ—yā vā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā upanikkhittā, ayaṁ vā mahāpathavī”ti?
root_text.sn56.55:1.4: “Etadeva, bhante, bahutaraṁ, yadidaṁ—mahāpathavī; appamattikā satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā upanikkhittā.
root_text.sn56.55:1.5: Saṅkhampi na upenti, upanidhampi na upenti, kalabhāgampi na upenti mahāpathaviṁ upanidhāya satta kolaṭṭhimattiyo guḷikā upanikkhittā”ti.

translation_text.sn56.55:1.1: “Mendicants, suppose a person was to place seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds on the great earth.
translation_text.sn56.55:1.3: Which is more: the seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds, or the great earth?”
translation_text.sn56.55:1.4: “Sir, the great earth is certainly more. The seven clay balls the size of jujube seeds are tiny.
translation_text.sn56.55:1.5: Compared to the great earth, they don’t count, there’s no comparison, they’re not worth a fraction.”
translation_text.sn56.55:1.6: “In the same way, for a noble disciple …
translation_text.sn56.55:1.7: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
